sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with right knee pain after playing basketball, where he felt a pop in his knee and experienced swelling, making it difficult to walk.
He reports the pain is achy and located on the inside of his knee.
The patient has been taking Tylenol, which provided some relief.
The swelling has decreased since he slept overnight.
The patient rates his pain level as a 4 out of 10.
He is currently favoring his knee and is able to bear weight on it.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and diabetes.
He is taking Norvasc 5mg daily for hypertension and Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
However, he often skips his Metformin due to gastrointestinal side effects.
Review of systems: The patient's hypertension has been in the range of 140/80 to 150/80.
He has not been taking his blood sugars regularly, with last readings around 150.
Current medications:
Norvasc 5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned
Physical exam: The patient has pain to palpation on the medial aspect of the right knee, with some edema, swelling, and ecchymosis.
The varus and valgus tests are negative.
The patient has a grade 2 systolic ejection murmur.
Test Results: The patient had a normal x-ray of the right knee, with no fractures or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a possible MCL strain, which will require an MRI to confirm.
Problem list: Hypertension confirmed; diabetes confirmed; MCL strain suspected; MCL tear possible; past medical history of grade 2 systolic ejection murmur.
Plan:
I prescribed Meloxicam 15mg to help with pain and swelling.
I will put the patient in a knee immobilizer and have him stay off the knee for the next couple of weeks.
I will order an MRI to confirm the MCL strain or tear.
I will also prescribe a knee immobilizer and instruct the patient to continue taking Tylenol as needed.
For hypertension, I will increase Norvasc 5mg and adjust the medication as needed.
I will also prescribe a consult to nutrition to help improve the patient's diet.
I will see the patient in two weeks.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in two weeks to follow up on his progress and adjust his treatment plan as needed.
